title: [BUG] Can't create columns in typescript
author: Gennier

comments.author: dhruvpsaru
comments.body:
Seems like it was introduced in version `4.2` as  we have `columns: Array<Column>;`
I checked in 4.1 it was 	`columns: Array<Partial<Column>>` in the type definition file.

comments.author: ccarstens
comments.body: Thank you @dhruvpsaru, we're using version 4.1 now until there is a fix!

comments.author: vchouhan
comments.body: Is this issue resolved in 4.2? `ws.columns = sheet.content.headers;` `TS2322: Type 'Headers[]' is not assignable to type 'Column[]'.   Type 'Headers' is not assignable to type 'Column'.     Property 'outlineLevel' is optional in type 'Headers' but required in type 'Column’.`

comments.author: ccarstens
comments.body: @vchouhan the issue was, according to @dhruvpsaru, introduced in v4.2. If you don't depend on features or improvements added in v4.2 you can simply use 4.1, so far that's working for me.

comments.author: KingDarBoja
liuweiGL
Artaud
comments.body:
> You can cast if you are using 4.2 in the meantime:
> 
> ```ts
> const workbook = new Workbook();
> workbook.creator = workbookName;
> 
> const worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet(workSheetName);
> worksheet.columns = [
>     { header: 'Id', key: 'id', width: 10 },
>     { header: 'Name', key: 'name', width: 32 },
>     { header: 'D.O.B.', key: 'DOB', width: 10, outlineLevel: 1 },
> ] as as ExcelProper.Column[]; // :)
> ```

Where is the ExcelProper coming from @KingDarBoja ? I don't see it in this lib.

comments.author: Aidam1
petrzjunior
comments.body: Seems to be duplicate of #1517. The type used to be `Partial`, which was removed in 4.2 and added back in PR #1518, GitHub says it is part of 4.2.1 tag. TS does no longer complain on my machine.
